paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The south coast of Peru is a narrow, 2,000-kilometre-long strip of desert squeezed between the Andes and the Pacific Ocean. It is also one of the most fragile ecosystems on Earth. It hardly ever rains there, and the only year-round source of water is located tens of metres below the surface.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: This is why the huarango tree is so suited to life there: it has the longest roots of any tree in the world. They stretch down 50–80 metres and, as well as sucking up water for the tree, they bring it into the higher subsoil, creating a water source for other plant life.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Dr David Beresford-Jones, archaeobotanist at Cambridge University, has been studying the role of the huarango tree in landscape change in the Lower Ica Valley in southern Peru. He believes the huarango was key to the ancient people's diet and, because it could reach deep water sources, it allowed local people to withstand years of drought when their other crops failed. But over the centuries huarango trees were gradually replaced with crops. Cutting down native woodland leads to erosion, as there is nothing to keep the soil in place. So when the huarangos go, the land turns into a desert. Nothing grows at all in the Lower Ica Valley now.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: For centuries the huarango tree was vital to the people of the neighbouring Middle Ica Valley too. They grew vegetables under it and ate products made from its seed pods. Its leaves and bark were used for herbal remedies, while its branches were used for charcoal for cooking and heating, and its trunk was used to build houses. But now it is disappearing rapidly. The majority of the huarango forests in the valley have already been cleared for fuel and agriculture – initially, these were smallholdings, but now they're huge farms producing crops for the international market.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: 'Of the forests that were here 1,000 years ago, 99 per cent have already gone,' says botanist Oliver Whaley from Kew Gardens in London, who, together with ethnobotanist Dr William Milliken, is running a pioneering project to protect and restore the rapidly disappearing habitat. In order to succeed, Whaley needs to get the local people on board, and that has meant overcoming local prejudices. 'Increasingly aspirational communities think that if you plant food trees in your home or street, it shows you are poor, and still need to grow your own food,' he says. In order to stop the Middle Ica Valley going the same way as the Lower Ica Valley, Whaley is encouraging locals to love the huarangos again. 'It's a process of cultural resuscitation,' he says. He has already set up a huarango festival to reinstate a sense of pride in their eco-heritage, and has helped local schoolchildren plant thousands of trees.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: 'In order to get people interested in habitat restoration, you need to plant a tree that is useful to them,' says Whaley. So, he has been working with local families to attempt to create a sustainable income from the huarangos by turning their products into foodstuffs. 'Boil up the beans and you get this thick brown syrup like molasses. You can also use it in drinks, soups or stews.' The pods can be ground into flour to make cakes, and the seeds roasted into a sweet, chocolatey 'coffee'. 'It's packed full of vitamins and minerals,' Whaley says.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: And some farmers are already planting huarangos. Alberto Benevides, owner of Ica Valley's only certified organic farm, which Whaley helped set up, has been planting the tree for 13 years. He produces syrup and flour, and sells these products at an organic farmers' market in Lima. His farm is relatively small and doesn't yet provide him with enough to live on, but he hopes this will change. 'The organic market is growing rapidly in Peru,' Benevides says. 'I am investing in the future.'

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: But even if Whaley can convince the local people to fall in love with the huarango again, there is still the threat of the larger farms. Some of these cut across the forests and break up the corridors that allow the essential movement of mammals, birds and pollen up and down the narrow forest strip. In the hope of counteracting this, he's persuading farmers to let him plant forest corridors on their land. He believes the extra woodland will also benefit the farms by reducing their water usage through a lowering of evaporation and providing a refuge for bio-control insects.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: 'If we can record biodiversity and see how it all works, then we're in a good position to move on from there. Desert habitats can reduce down to very little,' Whaley explains. 'It's not like a rainforest that needs to have this huge expanse. Life has always been confined to corridors and islands here. If you just have a few trees left, the population can grow up quickly because it's used to exploiting water when it arrives.' He sees his project as a model that has the potential to be rolled out across other arid areas around the world. 'If we can do it here, in the most fragile system on Earth, then that's a real message of hope for lots of places, including Africa, where there is drought and they just can't afford to wait for rain.'

content.title: Silbo Gomero – the whistle 'language' of the Canary Islands

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: La Gomera is one of the Canary Islands situated in the Atlantic Ocean off the northwest coast of Africa. This small volcanic island is mountainous, with steep rocky slopes and deep, wooded ravines, rising to 1,487 metres at its highest peak. It is also home to the best known of the world's whistle 'languages', a means of transmitting information over long distances which is perfectly adapted to the extreme terrain of the island.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: This 'language', known as 'Silbo' or 'Silbo Gomero' – from the Spanish word for 'whistle' – is now shedding light on the language-processing abilities of the human brain, according to scientists. Researchers say that Silbo activates parts of the brain normally associated with spoken language, suggesting that the brain is remarkably flexible in its ability to interpret sounds as language.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: 'Science has developed the idea of brain areas that are dedicated to language, and we are starting to understand the scope of signals that can be recognised as language,' says David Corina, co-author of a recent study and associate professor of psychology at the University of Washington in Seattle.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Silbo is a substitute for Spanish, with individual words recoded into whistles which have high- and low-frequency tones. A whistler – or silbador – puts a finger in his or her mouth to increase the whistle's pitch, while the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound. 'There is much more ambiguity in the whistled signal than in the spoken signal,' explains lead researcher Manuel Carreiras, psychology professor at the University of La Laguna on the Canary island of Tenerife. Because whistled 'words' can be hard to distinguish, silbadores rely on repetition, as well as awareness of context, to make themselves understood.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The silbadores of Gomera are traditionally shepherds and other isolated mountain folk, and their novel means of staying in touch allows them to communicate over distances of up to 10 kilometres. Carreiras explains that silbadores are able to pass a surprising amount of information via their whistles. 'In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands, but any Spanish sentence could be whistled.' Silbo has proved particularly useful when fires have occurred on the island and rapid communication across large areas has been vital.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The study team used neuroimaging equipment to contrast the brain activity of silbadores while listening to whistled and spoken Spanish. Results showed the left temporal lobe of the brain, which is usually associated with spoken language, was engaged during the processing of Silbo. The researchers found that other key regions in the brain's frontal lobe also responded to the whistles, including those activated in response to sign language among deaf people. When the experiments were repeated with non-whistlers, however, activation was observed in all areas of the brain.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: 'Our results provide more evidence about the flexibility of human capacity for language in a variety of forms,' Corina says. 'These data suggest that left-hemisphere language regions are uniquely adapted for communicative purposes, independent of the modality of signal. The non-Silbo speakers were not recognising Silbo as a language. They had nothing to grab onto, so multiple areas of their brains were activated.'

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Carreiras says the origins of Silbo Gomero remain obscure, but that indigenous Canary Islanders, who were of North African origin, already had a whistled language when Spain conquered the volcanic islands in the 15th century. Whistled languages survive today in Papua New Guinea, Mexico, Vietnam, Guyana, China, Nepal, Senegal, and a few mountainous pockets in southern Europe. There are thought to be as many as 70 whistled languages still in use, though only 12 have been described and studied scientifically. This form of communication is an adaptation found among cultures where people are often isolated from each other, according to Julien Meyer, a researcher at the Institute of Human Sciences in Lyon, France. 'They are mostly used in mountains or dense forests,' he says. 'Whistled languages are quite clearly defined and represent an original adaptation of the spoken language for the needs of isolated human groups.'

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: But with modern communication technology now widely available, researchers say whistled languages like Silbo are threatened with extinction. With dwindling numbers of Gomera islanders still fluent in the language, Canaries' authorities are taking steps to try to ensure its survival.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: Since 1999, Silbo Gomero has been taught in all of the island's elementary schools. In addition, locals are seeking assistance from the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO). 'The local authorities are trying to get an award from the organisation to declare [Silbo Gomero] as something that should be preserved for humanity,' Carreiras adds.

questions.title: La Gomera is the most mountainous of all the Canary Islands.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "La Gomera is the most mountainous of all the Canary Islands" 在文章中并没有明确提到。虽然文章提到 La Gomera 是一个多山的岛屿，但并没有提到它是所有加那利群岛中最山地的岛屿。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到明确的对应，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: La Gomera is one of the Canary Islands situated in the Atlantic Ocean off the northwest coast of Africa. This small volcanic island is mountainous, with steep rocky slopes and deep, wooded ravines, rising to 1,487 metres at its highest peak.
central_sentences.translation: 拉戈梅拉是位于非洲西北海岸大西洋上的加那利群岛之一。这个小火山岛多山，有陡峭的岩石斜坡和深深的树木茂盛的峡谷，其最高峰海拔1,487米。

This small volcanic island is mountainous, with steep rocky slopes and deep, wooded ravines, rising to 1,487 metres at its highest peak.
La Gomera is one of the Canary Islands situated in the Atlantic Ocean off the northwest coast of Africa.

questions.title: Silbo is only appropriate for short and simple messages.
questions.analysis: 题干中的说法是“Silbo 只适用于短的和简单的信息”，而原文明确指出“任何西班牙语句子都可以用哨声传递”，这表明 Silbo 并不仅限于短的和简单的信息。因此，题干的说法是错误的，正确答案为 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands, but any Spanish sentence could be whistled.
central_sentences.translation: 在日常生活中，他们使用哨声传达简短的命令，但任何西班牙语句子都可以用哨声表达。

central_sentences.sentence: Carreiras explains that silbadores are able to pass a surprising amount of information via their whistles.
central_sentences.translation: Carreiras解释说，吹哨者能够通过哨声传递令人惊讶的大量信息。

target_sen.target_sen: Silbo has proved particularly useful when fires have occurred on the island and rapid communication across large areas has been vital.
Silbo has proved particularly useful when fires have occurred on the island and rapid communication across large areas has been vital.
'In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands, but any Spanish sentence could be whistled.'
'In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands, but any Spanish sentence could be whistled.'

questions.title: In the brain–activity study, silbadores and non-whistlers produced different results.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“brain–activity study”对应原文中的“neuroimaging equipment”和“brain activity”；“silbadores”和“non-whistlers”在原文中直接提到；“different results”对应原文中的“activation was observed in all areas of the brain”和“left temporal lobe of the brain was engaged”。原文明确指出了两组人在脑活动研究中的不同结果，因此答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Results showed the left temporal lobe of the brain, which is usually associated with spoken language, was engaged during the processing of Silbo. The researchers found that other key regions in the brain's frontal lobe also responded to the whistles, including those activated in response to sign language among deaf people. When the experiments were repeated with non-whistlers, however, activation was observed in all areas of the brain.
central_sentences.translation: 结果显示，通常与口语相关的大脑左颞叶在处理Silbo（口哨语）时被激活。研究人员发现，大脑前叶的其他关键区域也对口哨做出反应，包括那些在聋人使用手语时被激活的区域。然而，当对非口哨使用者重复进行实验时，观察到大脑的所有区域都被激活。

syn_ary.synonym: neuroimaging equipment to contrast the brain activity

target_sen.target_sen: The study team used neuroimaging equipment to contrast the brain activity of silbadores while listening to whistled and spoken Spanish.
The study team used neuroimaging equipment to contrast the brain activity of silbadores while listening to whistled and spoken Spanish.
When the experiments were repeated with non-whistlers, however, activation was observed in all areas of the brain.

syn_ary.synonym: activation was observed in all areas of the brain

target_sen.target_sen: When the experiments were repeated with non-whistlers, however, activation was observed in all areas of the brain.

questions.title: The Spanish introduced Silbo to the islands in the 15th century.
questions.analysis: 题干中的陈述是“西班牙人在15世纪引入了Silbo”，而原文明确指出在西班牙人征服这些岛屿之前，当地的土著居民已经有了一种口哨语言。这与题干的陈述相矛盾，因此正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Carreiras says the origins of Silbo Gomero remain obscure, but that indigenous Canary Islanders, who were of North African origin, already had a whistled language when Spain conquered the volcanic islands in the 15th century.
central_sentences.translation: Carreiras说，Silbo Gomero的起源仍然不明确，但当西班牙在15世纪征服这些火山岛时，来自北非的加那利群岛土著居民已经有了一种口哨语言。

target_sen.target_sen: Carreiras says the origins of Silbo Gomero remain obscure, but that indigenous Canary Islanders, who were of North African origin, already had a whistled language when Spain conquered the volcanic islands in the 15th century.

Carreiras says the origins of Silbo Gomero remain obscure, but that indigenous Canary Islanders, who were of North African origin, already had a whistled language when Spain conquered the volcanic islands in the 15th century.

questions.title: There is precise data available regarding all of the whistle languages in existence today.
questions.analysis: 题干声称所有的哨语都有精确的数据（precise data），而原文明确指出只有12种哨语被科学描述和研究过（described and studied scientifically），这与题干中的“所有的哨语”（all of the whistle languages）形成了直接对比。因此，题干信息是错误的，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: There are thought to be as many as 70 whistled languages still in use, though only 12 have been described and studied scientifically.
central_sentences.translation: 据认为，现在仍在使用的哨语大约有70种，但只有12种得到了科学的描述和研究。

target_sen.target_sen: There are thought to be as many as 70 whistled languages still in use, though only 12 have been described and studied scientifically.
There are thought to be as many as 70 whistled languages still in use, though only 12 have been described and studied scientifically.

questions.title: The children of Gomera now learn Silbo.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "The children of Gomera now learn Silbo" 可以在文章中找到对应的表达。题干中的 "children" 对应文章中的 "elementary schools"，"learn" 对应 "taught"，"Silbo" 对应 "Silbo Gomero"。因此，题干中的信息在文章中得到了确认，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Since 1999, Silbo Gomero has been taught in all of the island's elementary schools.
central_sentences.translation: 自1999年以来，戈梅拉岛的所有小学都开始教授西尔博语。

target_sen.target_sen: Since 1999, Silbo Gomero has been taught in all of the island's elementary schools.
Since 1999, Silbo Gomero has been taught in all of the island's elementary schools.
Since 1999, Silbo Gomero has been taught in all of the island's elementary schools.
'The local authorities are trying to get an award from the organisation to declare [Silbo Gomero] as something that should be preserved for humanity,' Carreiras adds.

groups.title: Silbo Gomero
groups.desc:
Questions 20–26
Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 20–26 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: How Silbo is produced • high- and low-frequency tones represent different sounds in Spanish$614$ • pitch of whistle is controlled using silbador's$615$ • $616$is changed with a cupped hand How Silbo is used • has long been used by shepherds and people living in secluded locations • in everyday use for the transmission of brief$617$ • can relay essential information quickly, e.g. to inform people about$618$ The future of Silbo • future under threat because of new$619$ • Canaries' authorities hoping to receive a UNESCO$620$to help preserve it

questions.analysis: 题干中的“high-and low-frequency tones”在原文中直接对应“high- and low-frequency tones”，而“different sounds in Spanish”对应“individual words”。因此，题干中的空格应填入“words”，因为这是原文中明确指出的高频和低频音调所代表的内容。

central_sentences.sentence: Silbo is a substitute for Spanish, with individual words recoded into whistles which have high- and low-frequency tones.
central_sentences.translation: Silbo是西班牙语的一种替代形式，将单个词重新编码成具有高频和低频音调的哨音。

target_sen.target_sen: Silbo is a substitute for Spanish, with individual words recoded into whistles which have high- and low-frequency tones.
Silbo is a substitute for Spanish, with individual words recoded into whistles which have high- and low-frequency tones.

questions.analysis: 题干问的是“silbador 用什么控制哨声的音调（pitch）”，而文章中明确提到“silbador 将手指放入口中以提高哨声的音调（puts a finger in his or her mouth to increase the whistle's pitch）”。因此，答案是“finger”。

central_sentences.sentence: A whistler – or silbador – puts a finger in his or her mouth to increase the whistle's pitch, while the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound.
central_sentences.translation: 吹口哨的人（或称silbador）将手指放入口中以提高口哨的音调，同时可以用另一只手弯曲成杯状来调整声音的方向。

target_sen.target_sen: A whistler – or silbador – puts a finger in his or her mouth to increase the whistle's pitch, while the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound.
A whistler – or silbador – puts a finger in his or her mouth to increase the whistle's pitch, while the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "cupped hand" 和 "changed" 在原文中对应 "cupped" 和 "adjust"。原文句子 "the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound" 说明了用弯曲的手来调整声音的方向。因此，答案是 "direction"。

central_sentences.sentence: A whistler – or silbador – puts a finger in his or her mouth to increase the whistle's pitch, while the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound.
central_sentences.translation: 口哨者（或称silbador）将一根手指放入口中以提高哨音的音调，同时另一只手可以做成杯状来调整声音的方向。

target_sen.target_sen: A whistler – or silbador – puts a finger in his or her mouth to increase the whistle's pitch, while the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound.
A whistler – or silbador – puts a finger in his or her mouth to increase the whistle's pitch, while the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "everyday use" 在原文中对应 "In daily life"，而 "brief" 对应 "short"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以定位到答案句 "In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands..."。因此，答案是 "commands"，因为它符合题干中传达简短信息的要求。

central_sentences.sentence: In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands, but any Spanish sentence could be whistled.
central_sentences.translation: 在日常生活中，他们使用口哨来传达简短的命令，但任何西班牙语句子都可以被口哨化。

target_sen.target_sen: 'In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands, but any Spanish sentence could be whistled.'
'In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands, but any Spanish sentence could be whistled.'

questions.analysis: 题干中的“inform people about”意思是“告知人们关于什么”。在原文中，最直接对应的句子是“Silbo has proved particularly useful when fires have occurred on the island and rapid communication across large areas has been vital.” 这句中的“fires”直接回答了“inform people about什么”。因此，答案是“fires”。

central_sentences.sentence: Silbo has proved particularly useful when fires have occurred on the island and rapid communication across large areas has been vital.
central_sentences.translation: 当岛上发生火灾，需要在大范围内进行快速沟通时，Silbo（口哨语）已被证明特别有用。

target_sen.target_sen: The silbadores of Gomera are traditionally shepherds and other isolated mountain folk, and their novel means of staying in touch allows them to communicate over distances of up to 10 kilometres.
'In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands, but any Spanish sentence could be whistled.'
The silbadores of Gomera are traditionally shepherds and other isolated mountain folk, and their novel means of staying in touch allows them to communicate over distances of up to 10 kilometres.
Carreiras explains that silbadores are able to pass a surprising amount of information via their whistles.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“future under threat”对应原文中的“threatened with extinction”，“new”对应原文中的“modern”。原文句子明确指出，现代通信技术的广泛应用使得口哨语言面临灭绝的威胁。因此，答案是“technology”。

central_sentences.sentence: But with modern communication technology now widely available, researchers say whistled languages like Silbo are threatened with extinction.
central_sentences.translation: 但随着现代通讯技术现在广泛可用，研究人员表示，像Silbo这样的口哨语言正面临灭绝的威胁。

target_sen.target_sen: But with modern communication technology now widely available, researchers say whistled languages like Silbo are threatened with extinction.
But with modern communication technology now widely available, researchers say whistled languages like Silbo are threatened with extinction.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "hoping to receive" 对应文章中的 "trying to get"，"UNESCO" 在文章中也直接提到，"preserve" 对应文章中的 "preserved"。这些对应关系帮助我们定位到答案句。答案句中明确提到地方当局希望从 UNESCO 获得一个 "award"（奖项），以帮助保护 Silbo Gomero。因此，正确答案是 "award"。

central_sentences.sentence: 'The local authorities are trying to get an award from the organisation to declare [Silbo Gomero] as something that should be preserved for humanity,' Carreiras adds.
central_sentences.translation: Carreiras补充说："当地政府正在努力从该组织获得一个奖项，以宣布[戈梅拉岛哨语]应该作为人类遗产得到保护。"

target_sen.target_sen: In addition, locals are seeking assistance from the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO).

syn_ary.synonym: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization

target_sen.target_sen: In addition, locals are seeking assistance from the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO).
'The local authorities are trying to get an award from the organisation to declare [Silbo Gomero] as something that should be preserved for humanity,' Carreiras adds.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The environmental practices of big businesses are shaped by a fundamental fact that for many of us offends our sense of justice. Depending on the circumstances, a business may maximize the amount of money it makes, at least in the short term, by damaging the environment and hurting people. That is still the case today for fishermen in an unmanaged fishery without quotas, and for international logging companies with short-term leases on tropical rainforest land in places with corrupt officials and unsophisticated landowners. When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: It is easy for the rest of us to blame a business for helping itself by hurting other people. But blaming alone is unlikely to produce change. It ignores the fact that businesses are not charities but profit-making companies, and that publicly owned companies with shareholders are under obligation to those shareholders to maximize profits, provided that they do so by legal means. US laws make a company's directors legally liable for something termed 'breach of fiduciary responsibility' if they knowingly manage a company in a way that reduces profits. The car manufacturer Henry Ford was in fact successfully sued by shareholders in 1919 for raising the minimum wage of his workers to $5 per day: the courts declared that, while Ford's humanitarian sentiments about his employees were nice, his business existed to make profits for its stockholders.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Our blaming of businesses also ignores the ultimate responsibility of the public for creating the conditions that let a business profit through destructive environmental policies. In the long run, it is the public, either directly or through its politicians, that has the power to make such destructive policies unprofitable and illegal, and to make sustainable environmental policies profitable.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The public can do that by suing businesses for harming them, as happened after the Exxon Valdez disaster, in which over 40,000 m3 of oil were spilled off the coast of Alaska. The public may also make their opinion felt by preferring to buy sustainably harvested products; by making employees of companies with poor track records feel ashamed of their company and complain to their own management; by preferring their governments to award valuable contracts to businesses with a good environmental track record; and by pressing their governments to pass and enforce laws and regulations requiring good environmental practices.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: In turn, big businesses can exert powerful pressure on any suppliers that might ignore public or government pressure. For instance, after the US public became concerned about the spread of a disease known as BSE, which was transmitted to humans through infected meat, the US government's Food and Drug Administration introduced rules demanding that the meat industry abandon practices associated with the risk of the disease spreading. But for five years the meat packers refused to follow these, claiming that they would be too expensive to obey. However, when a major fast-food company then made the same demands after customer purchases of its hamburgers plummeted, the meat industry complied within weeks. The public's task is therefore to identify which links in the supply chain are sensitive to public pressure: for instance, fast-food chains or jewelry stores, but not meat packers or gold miners.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Some readers may be disappointed or outraged that I place the ultimate responsibility for business practices harming the public on the public itself. I also believe that the public must accept the necessity for higher prices for products to cover the added costs, if any, of sound environmental practices. My views may seem to ignore the belief that businesses should act in accordance with moral principles even if this leads to a reduction in their profits. But I think we have to recognize that, throughout human history, in all politically complex human societies, government regulation has arisen precisely because it was found that not only did moral principles need to be made explicit, they also needed to be enforced.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: To me, the conclusion that the public has the ultimate responsibility for the behavior of even the biggest businesses is empowering and hopeful, rather than disappointing. My conclusion is not a moralistic one about who is right or wrong, admirable or selfish, a good guy or a bad guy. In the past, businesses have changed when the public came to expect and require different behavior, to reward businesses for behavior that the public wanted, and to make things difficult for businesses practicing behaviors that the public didn't want. I predict that in the future, just as in the past, changes in public attitudes will be essential for changes in businesses' environmental practices.

groups.title: Big businesses

Questions 27–31
Complete the summary using the list of words, A–J, below.

groups.collect: Many big businesses today are prepared to harm people and the environment in order to make money, and they appear to have no$621$. Lack of$622$by governments and lack of public$623$ can lead to environmental problems such as$624$ or the destruction of$625$

questions.analysis: 题干提到企业为了赚钱会伤害人们和环境，并且没有某种约束。文章中提到企业为了最大化利润会忽略道德标准（moral standards），这与题干中的“no”相对应。因此，正确答案是D: moral standards。

central_sentences.sentence: Depending on the circumstances, a business may maximize the amount of money it makes, at least in the short term, by damaging the environment and hurting people.
central_sentences.translation: 根据具体情况，一个企业可能会通过破坏环境和伤害人们来最大化短期利润。

target_sen.target_sen: Depending on the circumstances, a business may maximize the amount of money it makes, at least in the short term, by damaging the environment and hurting people.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“Lack of【 】by governments”对应原文中的“government regulation is ineffective”。这里的“ineffective”表示“无效的”，与“Lack of”意思相同。政府缺乏有效的监管会导致环境问题，因此正确答案是“control”。

central_sentences.sentence: When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.
central_sentences.translation: 当政府监管有效，且公众具有环保意识时，环保的大企业可能会胜过不环保的企业；但如果政府监管无效，且公众不关心环境问题，情况可能就会相反。

target_sen.target_sen: When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.

When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.
Depending on the circumstances, a business may maximize the amount of money it makes, at least in the short term, by damaging the environment and hurting people.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“lack of by governments”和“lack of public”分别对应原文中的“government regulation is ineffective”和“public doesn't care”。这表明政府监管无效和公众不关心会导致环境问题。因此，正确答案是“involvement”，因为它与“public doesn't care”同义。

central_sentences.sentence: When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.

target_sen.target_sen: When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.

When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“lack of by governments”和“lack of public”分别对应原文中的“government regulation is ineffective”和“if the public doesn't care”。原文提到“unmanaged fishery without quotas”导致了“overfishing”，这与题干中的“environmental problems”相对应。因此，正确答案是“overfishing”。

central_sentences.sentence: That is still the case today for fishermen in an unmanaged fishery without quotas, and for international logging companies with short-term leases on tropical rainforest land in places with corrupt officials and unsophisticated landowners.

target_sen.target_sen: When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.

When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.

That is still the case today for fishermen in an unmanaged fishery without quotas, and for international logging companies with short-term leases on tropical rainforest land in places with corrupt officials and unsophisticated landowners.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“destruction of”最直接对应的是文章中的“international logging companies with short-term leases on tropical rainforest land”，因为文章明确提到了伐木公司对热带雨林的破坏，而热带雨林主要由树木组成。因此，正确答案是“trees”。

central_sentences.sentence: That is still the case today for fishermen in an unmanaged fishery without quotas, and for international logging companies with short-term leases on tropical rainforest land in places with corrupt officials and unsophisticated landowners.
central_sentences.translation: 这种情况在今天仍然存在，比如在没有配额管理的渔业中的渔民，以及在有腐败官员和缺乏经验的土地所有者的地方，对热带雨林土地进行短期租赁的国际伐木公司。

target_sen.target_sen: When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.

When government regulation is effective, and when the public is environmentally aware, environmentally clean big businesses may out-compete dirty ones, but the reverse is likely to be true if government regulation is ineffective and if the public doesn't care.

That is still the case today for fishermen in an unmanaged fishery without quotas, and for international logging companies with short-term leases on tropical rainforest land in places with corrupt officials and unsophisticated landowners.

groups.desc:
Questions 32–34

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 32–34 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: The main idea of the third paragraph is that environmental damage

options.text: requires political action if it is to be stopped.
is the result of ignorance on the part of the public.
could be prevented by the action of ordinary people.
can only be stopped by educating business leaders.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C，因为文章第三段的主要意思是公众有能力通过自己的行动来防止环境破坏。文章明确指出，公众有责任和力量改变政策，使破坏性政策无利可图并使可持续政策有利可图。这与选项C中的“普通人的行动可以防止环境破坏”最为接近。

central_sentences.sentence: In the long run, it is the public, either directly or through its politicians, that has the power to make such destructive policies unprofitable and illegal, and to make sustainable environmental policies profitable.
central_sentences.translation: 从长远来看，公众可以直接或通过其政治家，有能力使这些破坏性政策变得无利可图和非法，并使可持续的环境政策变得有利可图。

target_sen.target_sen: Our blaming of businesses also ignores the ultimate responsibility of the public for creating the conditions that let a business profit through destructive environmental policies.

questions.title: In the fourth paragraph, the writer describes ways in which the public can

options.text: reduce their own individual impact on the environment.
learn more about the impact of business on the environment.
raise awareness of the effects of specific environmental disasters.
influence the environmental policies of businesses and governments.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D，因为文章中详细描述了公众如何通过各种方式影响企业和政府的环境政策。这些方式包括起诉企业、选择购买可持续产品、让员工感到羞耻、希望政府将合同授予有良好环境记录的企业，以及敦促政府通过和执行环境法律和法规。这些都表明公众可以通过这些方式影响企业和政府的环境政策。

central_sentences.sentence: The public can do that by suing businesses for harming them, as happened after the Exxon Valdez disaster, in which over 40,000 m3 of oil were spilled off the coast of Alaska. The public may also make their opinion felt by preferring to buy sustainably harvested products; by making employees of companies with poor track records feel ashamed of their company and complain to their own management; by preferring their governments to award valuable contracts to businesses with a good environmental track record; and by pressing their governments to pass and enforce laws and regulations requiring good environmental practices.
central_sentences.translation: 公众可以通过起诉对他们造成伤害的企业来实现这一点，就像在埃克森·瓦尔迪兹灾难之后发生的那样，当时超过40,000立方米的石油泄漏到了阿拉斯加海岸。公众还可以通过以下方式表达他们的观点：选择购买可持续采购的产品；让环境记录不佳的公司员工为其公司感到羞耻并向管理层投诉；倾向于让政府将有价值的合同授予环境记录良好的企业；以及敦促政府通过和执行要求良好环境实践的法律和法规。

questions.title: What pressure was exerted by big business in the case of the disease BSE?

options.text: Meat packers stopped supplying hamburgers to fast-food chains.
A fast-food company forced their meat suppliers to follow the law.
Meat packers persuaded the government to reduce their expenses.
A fast-food company encouraged the government to introduce legislation.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 B。文章中明确提到，当一家主要的快餐公司对其肉类供应商提出相同的要求时，肉类行业在几周内就遵从了。这表明快餐公司迫使其肉类供应商遵守法律规定。

central_sentences.sentence: However, when a major fast-food company then made the same demands after customer purchases of its hamburgers plummeted, the meat industry complied within weeks.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，当一家大型快餐公司在其汉堡销量暴跌后提出同样的要求时，肉类行业在几周内就遵守了。

target_sen.target_sen: However, when a major fast-food company then made the same demands after customer purchases of its hamburgers plummeted, the meat industry complied within weeks.
However, when a major fast-food company then made the same demands after customer purchases of its hamburgers plummeted, the meat industry complied within weeks.

For instance, after the US public became concerned about the spread of a disease known as BSE, which was transmitted to humans through infected meat, the US government's Food and Drug Administration introduced rules demanding that the meat industry abandon practices associated with the risk of the disease spreading.

groups.desc:
Questions 35–39
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 35–39 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: The public should be prepared to fund good environmental practices.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "The public should be prepared to fund good environmental practices" 可以在文章中找到对应的表达。文章中提到 "the public must accept the necessity for higher prices for products to cover the added costs, if any, of sound environmental practices"，这与题干的意思相符。题干中的 "fund" 对应文章中的 "cover the added costs"，而 "good environmental practices" 对应 "sound environmental practices"。因此，正确答案是 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: I also believe that the public must accept the necessity for higher prices for products to cover the added costs, if any, of sound environmental practices.
central_sentences.translation: 我也相信公众必须接受为产品支付更高价格的必要性，以支付良好环境实践可能带来的额外成本。

target_sen.target_sen: Some readers may be disappointed or outraged that I place the ultimate responsibility for business practices harming the public on the public itself.
I also believe that the public must accept the necessity for higher prices for products to cover the added costs, if any, of sound environmental practices.
I also believe that the public must accept the necessity for higher prices for products to cover the added costs, if any, of sound environmental practices.
I also believe that the public must accept the necessity for higher prices for products to cover the added costs, if any, of sound environmental practices.

questions.title: There is a contrast between the moral principles of different businesses.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词 "contrast"（对比）、"moral principles"（道德原则）和 "different businesses"（不同企业）在原文中没有找到明确的对应内容。文章主要讨论企业在环境保护方面的行为，以及公众和政府对企业行为的影响，但没有具体讨论企业之间在道德原则上的对比。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 原文未出现“different businesses”之间moral principles对比，故NOT GIVEN。
central_sentences.analysis: 文中谈利润义务与道德，但未比较不同企业道德差异。

questions.title: It is important to make a clear distinction between acceptable and unacceptable behaviour.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“明确区分可接受和不可接受的行为是重要的”，而原文中提到的是道德原则需要被明确（made explicit）和执行（enforced），并没有强调仅仅区分行为的重要性。因此，题干中的信息与原文内容不符，正确答案为 NO。

central_sentences.sentence: But I think we have to recognize that, throughout human history, in all politically complex human societies, government regulation has arisen precisely because it was found that not only did moral principles need to be made explicit, they also needed to be enforced.
central_sentences.translation: 但我认为我们必须认识到，纵观人类历史，在所有政治复杂的人类社会中，政府法规的出现恰恰是因为人们发现，道德原则不仅需要被明确化，还需要被执行。

target_sen.target_sen: But I think we have to recognize that, throughout human history, in all politically complex human societies, government regulation has arisen precisely because it was found that not only did moral principles need to be made explicit, they also needed to be enforced.
My views may seem to ignore the belief that businesses should act in accordance with moral principles even if this leads to a reduction in their profits.

But I think we have to recognize that, throughout human history, in all politically complex human societies, government regulation has arisen precisely because it was found that not only did moral principles need to be made explicit, they also needed to be enforced.

questions.title: The public have successfully influenced businesses in the past.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 YES，因为文章明确指出“在过去，当公众期望和要求不同的行为时，企业发生了变化”，这与题干中的“公众成功地影响了企业”是直接对应的。通过识别题干中的关键词 "public", "successfully influenced", 和 "in the past"，并在文章中找到相应的同义替换词，可以确定答案句所在的位置，从而得出正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: In the past, businesses have changed when the public came to expect and require different behavior, to reward businesses for behavior that the public wanted, and to make things difficult for businesses practicing behaviors that the public didn't want.
central_sentences.translation: 在过去，当公众开始期待和要求不同的行为，奖励公众想要的企业行为，并给那些公众不想要的行为的企业制造麻烦时，企业就发生了改变。

target_sen.target_sen: To me, the conclusion that the public has the ultimate responsibility for the behavior of even the biggest businesses is empowering and hopeful, rather than disappointing.

In the past, businesses have changed when the public came to expect and require different behavior, to reward businesses for behavior that the public wanted, and to make things difficult for businesses practicing behaviors that the public didn't want.
I predict that in the future, just as in the past, changes in public attitudes will be essential for changes in businesses' environmental practices.

In the past, businesses have changed when the public came to expect and require different behavior, to reward businesses for behavior that the public wanted, and to make things difficult for businesses practicing behaviors that the public didn't want.

In the past, businesses have changed when the public came to expect and require different behavior, to reward businesses for behavior that the public wanted, and to make things difficult for businesses practicing behaviors that the public didn't want.

questions.title: In the future, businesses will show more concern for the environment.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词 "In the future" 和 "more concern for the environment" 在原文中找到了对应的表达 "in the future" 和 "changes in businesses' environmental practices"。然而，原文并没有明确说明未来企业将会更加关注环境，只是提到公众态度的变化会影响企业的环境实践。因此，题干中的信息在原文中并没有明确给出，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: I predict that in the future, just as in the past, changes in public attitudes will be essential for changes in businesses' environmental practices.
central_sentences.translation: 我预测，将来就像过去一样，公众态度的变化将是企业环境实践变化的关键。

target_sen.target_sen: I predict that in the future, just as in the past, changes in public attitudes will be essential for changes in businesses' environmental practices.
To me, the conclusion that the public has the ultimate responsibility for the behavior of even the biggest businesses is empowering and hopeful, rather than disappointing.

In the past, businesses have changed when the public came to expect and require different behavior, to reward businesses for behavior that the public wanted, and to make things difficult for businesses practicing behaviors that the public didn't want.
I predict that in the future, just as in the past, changes in public attitudes will be essential for changes in businesses' environmental practices.
I predict that in the future, just as in the past, changes in public attitudes will be essential for changes in businesses' environmental practices.

groups.desc:
Question 40
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in box 40 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: What would be the best subheading for this passage?

options.text: Will the world survive the threat caused by big businesses?
How can big businesses be encouraged to be less driven by profit?
What environmental dangers are caused by the greed of businesses?
Are big businesses to blame for the damage they cause the environment?

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 D: Are big businesses to blame for the damage they cause the environment?。文章主要讨论了企业是否应为其对环境造成的破坏负责，并且提到公众和政府在其中的角色。通过识别关键词和理解段落主题句，学生可以更容易地找到正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: It is easy for the rest of us to blame a business for helping itself by hurting other people. But blaming alone is unlikely to produce change.
central_sentences.translation: 我们很容易责怪企业为了自身利益而伤害他人。但是单纯的责备不太可能带来改变。
